title: agent cli
subtitle: native macOS cockpit — real agents in real terminal panes, built on Ghostty's engine, none of the IDE chrome
loop: Open app → pick folder → real claude/codex in a real terminal pane (libghostty-vt) → tabs=projects, panes=agents, file rail, inline editor. Build our own, leveraging open-source components. libghostty-vt verified. v0 = ONE live pane.

cards.title: Render + input loop — the rock
cards.summary: Extend the parsing spike (which is proven): pty → libghostty-vt → CANVAS PAINT + KEYBOARD ROUNDTRIP in a real window. Proves the three things parsing didn't — rendering to pixels, input encoding back to the pty, and fullscreen-TUI correctness (claude's own alt-screen UI) at terminal speed. Do this BEFORE scaffolding the app.
cards.done: Type in a canvas-rendered pane running a real shell; output appears; arrow keys + ctrl-c work; claude's TUI renders correctly; no visible lag on fast output.

cards.title: Tauri 2 app scaffold
cards.summary: Tauri 2 + React/TS/Vite frontend + Rust backend. Native window, IPC channel wired backend↔frontend. Empty but real — the container the SPIKE-2 pipeline drops into.
cards.done: Native window opens; a message round-trips backend↔frontend over IPC; builds clean with the pinned Zig toolchain.

cards.title: One agent pane (v0 done)
cards.summary: Native folder picker → spawn claude in that cwd → one interactive terminal pane using the SPIKE-2 pipeline. Persist the last folder, reopen on relaunch. This IS v0.
cards.done: Open app → pick folder → run claude → type and see output, feels like a real terminal. Relaunch reopens the folder.

cards.title: Tabs = projects
cards.summary: Add/switch project folders as tabs, multiple projects in one window. Native folder-open per tab.
cards.done: Open 3 project folders as tabs; switch between them; each keeps its own state.

cards.title: Multiple panes per tab
cards.summary: Grid of terminal panes per tab, each its own pty/agent. Pane focus, new pane (n), close pane (x). Watch memory/CPU with N concurrent agents (see ARCHITECTURE.md).
cards.done: 3 agent panes visible at once in one tab, each interactive; n adds, x closes, focus moves.

cards.title: mono-ghost theme + session persistence
cards.summary: Apply the already-approved mono-ghost palette (ghostty/config has the values). Persist tabs + panes across restarts.
cards.done: App looks like the approved demo; relaunch restores tabs and panes.

cards.title: File rail + preview
cards.summary: Yazi-style file tree with inline preview, per the demo's left rail. Git status indicators.
cards.done: Rail shows the project tree; selecting a file previews it; dirty files marked.

cards.title: Inline editor pane (CodeMirror)
cards.summary: Double-click a file in the rail → CodeMirror 6 editor pane, inline in the grid, real syntax highlighting. This is the 'looks like VSCode's editor' requirement — built directly, not via code serve-web.
cards.done: Double-click a .tsx file → it opens in an editor pane with line numbers + syntax highlighting, editable, save works.

cards.title: Worktree helper
cards.summary: One keystroke: new pane = git worktree add + agent launched inside it, named and disposable. The 'multiple agents on one repo, parallel' workflow.
cards.done: From a project tab: new-worktree-agent in <5s; cleanup removes the worktree + branch.

shipped.title: OSS landscape survey + tool evaluations
shipped.summary: July 2026: surveyed the field (zellij, Superconductor, hashmark, cmux, Mux0, Supacode). zellij trialed then parked (static-config model, not 'open a folder'). Superconductor/hashmark/cmux evaluated in depth. All reference only — the decision is build-our-own.
shipped.done: Drove the build-our-own direction. Rejected options documented in PARKED.md/DECISIONS.md with reasons.

shipped.title: Editor-fidelity spike (helix)
shipped.summary: hx sample.tsx next to the VSCode screenshot — confirmed a real editor with tree-sitter highlighting reads right. Informs the EDITOR slice (now CodeMirror in-app, not a TUI editor).

shipped.title: Blind-spot audits (drift + fork decision)
shipped.summary: Two 16-framework /blind sweeps caught real drift: demo-replaced-the-trial, and an asymmetric 'cmux is best' verdict. Second audit corrected a false claim (cmux chrome IS themeable) and, with Jason's pushback, steered back to build-our-own. docs/blind-audit-*.html.
shipped.done: Findings logged in ERRORS.md; direction corrected.

shipped.title: libghostty-vt parsing spike — PASSED
shipped.summary: spike-ghostty-vt/: real pty (portable-pty) → real 'ls -la -G' → real ANSI codes → libghostty-vt Terminal::vt_write parses → cell-by-cell readback matches exactly. Proves the terminal ENGINE choice (Ghostty's real parser in a Rust backend, not xterm.js). Caught the Zig 0.15.2 pin requirement.
shipped.done: Compiles + runs correctly, verified 2026-07-07. Logged in DECISIONS.md.
